body.u: As I sit here memorysmemories of my younger days flash through my head I look back at my life from when I was sixteen. I left school all set to go to colledgecollege for a two year course in hairdressing and beauty work. Though two years past very quickly and were the start of my adult life. In though two years I found out just what it was like to live on your own. After I had come out of colledgecollege I had found a job in a quite busy salon on the out-skirts of London. I lived away from home but I visited my parents most weekends. I worked in the same salon for about four years I entered lots of hairdressing competition and found those help me along in my profession. By this time I was twenty-two and thinking about settling down. I had made quite a lot of friends, but had still kept in touch with my friends on the island. I had found it hard at eighteen to have no money but in the long run hairdressing paid off.

One day my life was completely changed the man I knew I was going to marry walk in to it. He came into the shop to have his hair cut. We naturally got talking and from then on it was all go. We got on very well and soon got engaged. We were engaged for two years and then decided to get married. By this time I was twenty-five. We had saved enough to put a deposit on a house. My husband worked as an engineer. We lived quite near to where I worked, so it was only a little way into London. We did have some wonderful nights out together. I gave up my work when I was expecting our first child. It was a boy and we named it after Chris, my husband.

My life had changed again now we didn’t go out very much only once a week together. My time was consumed with our baby boy. As soon as Chris started school I started my profession again, but instead of in the shop I did it at home. I used to make a bomb doing it at home. We had soon saved up enough money to move to a larger home. I was also now expecting our second child at the age of thirty-two. Our second child was a girl and we called it Marie. We decided this was our last child, one of each was enough.

The house we moved to was a three bedroomed semi-detached house. It was first large enough for our family. Chris grew up very fast so did Marie. The years flew by. Soon Chris was fifteen and Marie was ten. By now I was forty-seven. It was strange to see Chris take an interest in girls and start bringing them home. I knew that soon both Chris and Marie would just start their life while mine was nearly over. But I had lead a full life and did not regret getting old.
